en.cs.diff_subtitle: Four capabilities no other compliance platform offers at any price.
en.cs.diff_selfhosted_desc: Your data never leaves your servers. Vanta and Drata are cloud-only SaaS — your compliance data lives on their infrastructure.
en.cs.diff_free_desc: Apache 2.0 licensed. Competitors charge $15,000–$100,000/year. KSM is open source — download, run, and audit.
en.cs.diff_realtime_desc: Full 178-rule scan in 30 seconds, every 6 hours automatically. Vanta and Drata run periodic checks with hours-long delays.
en.cs.diff_auto_desc: KSM finds problems AND fixes them. Competitors only monitor — you still need engineers to remediate manually.
en.cs.save_desc: Compliance platforms charge $15,000–$100,000/year. Kytran Server Manager is open source and self-hosted.
en.membership.hero_subtitle: Everything you need. One platform. No tool sprawl.
en.membership.faq_a1: Each plan gives you access to a set of integrated business tools -- CRM, invoicing, content creation, and more. Higher tiers unlock additional modules like Website Designer, Social Media Management, and fleet compute. All plans include platform updates and access to the Trust Center.
en.membership.faq_a2: Plans are billed monthly. You can cancel anytime. Enterprise plans are billed annually with custom terms. There are no hidden fees -- the price you see is the price you pay.
en.membership.faq_a3: Yes. You can upgrade at any time and the difference is prorated. Downgrading takes effect at the start of your next billing cycle. Your data is always preserved when changing plans.
en.membership.faq_a4: We offer a 14-day money-back guarantee on all paid plans. If the platform is not the right fit, contact us within 14 days for a full refund -- no questions asked.
en.membership.faq_q5: How do the savings compare to buying separate tools?
en.membership.faq_a5: We calculated market averages from leading competitors: Salesforce for CRM, Vanta for compliance, Shopify Plus for e-commerce, and more. A Professional plan replaces $2,500+/mo in separate subscriptions for just $799/mo -- a 68% savings.
en.membership.final_cta_title: Not sure which plan is right for you?

en.comp.hero_subtitle: It takes 9+ tools to match what A.R.C.H.I.E. does in one platform
en.comp.group_gaps: Where Others Lead (We’re Working On It)
en.comp.trending_intro: These tools are blowing up on social media right now. Are they competitors? Let’s scan them.
en.comp.trend_openclaw_meta: Personal AI assistant • Chat apps • Single agent
en.comp.trend_openclaw_desc: The one that started the "claw" craze. A personal AI assistant that lives in your messaging apps (Telegram, WhatsApp, iMessage). Went viral, creator joining OpenAI. Simple, elegant, one agent doing one thing well.
en.comp.trend_openclaw_verdict: Brilliant personal assistant. Not an operations platform. Like comparing a Swiss Army knife to a command center.
en.comp.trend_picoclaw_desc: OpenClaw’s little sibling. Runs on a $10 microcontroller with just 10MB of RAM. Perfect for IoT, embedded systems, and proving AI can run anywhere. Impressive engineering, completely different mission.
en.comp.trend_picoclaw_verdict: Cool tech, opposite universe. PicoClaw fits in your pocket. A.R.C.H.I.E. runs your entire operation.
en.comp.trend_clawempire_meta: Gamified coding • Pixel art UI • Wraps existing tools
en.comp.trend_clawempire_desc: A pixel-art game layer on top of Claude Code and Codex CLI. You “conquer” coding tasks in a retro game interface. Fun, viral on TikTok, but it’s a skin — the actual AI is someone else’s.
en.comp.trend_clawempire_verdict: Great marketing, zero original AI. When the game ends, you’re still using Claude Code. A.R.C.H.I.E. IS the platform.
en.comp.trend_nemoclaw_desc: Nvidia’s enterprise version of OpenClaw, announced at GTC March 2026. Adds AI guardrails, governance, and safety — features that overlap with A.R.C.H.I.E.’s C.R.E.E.D. ethics system. Still alpha, but Nvidia backing makes it serious.
en.comp.trend_nemoclaw_verdict: The only one worth watching. Nvidia + governance is serious. But A.R.C.H.I.E.’s C.R.E.E.D. is already live and production-tested — NemoClaw is still alpha.
en.comp.trend_footer: Trending ≠ competing. Social media hype fades. Running systems don’t.
en.comp.trend_scandate: Last scanned: April 4, 2026 • Auto-updated by A.R.C.H.I.E. every 2 weeks
en.comp.cat_orch_desc: Libraries for building multi-agent systems. You write code, define roles, and wire agents together. Multi-agent is now table stakes — every major framework shipped it by early 2026.
en.comp.cat_orch_edge: These are libraries you build with. A.R.C.H.I.E. is a running system — GPU-aware routing, warm-boot lifecycle, physical security, and 39 autonomous background jobs out of the box. No assembly required.
en.comp.cat_orch_gap: LangGraph offers durable execution with checkpoints. Google’s A2A protocol enables cross-platform agent communication — A.R.C.H.I.E. doesn’t support either yet.
en.comp.cat_work_desc: Visual drag-and-drop platforms for connecting LLMs to tools and data sources. Strong for no-code teams. Dify leads with RAG and prompt orchestration.
en.comp.cat_work_edge: Workflow builders connect pipes. A.R.C.H.I.E. manages the entire factory — agents, models, hardware, security, and operations.
en.comp.cat_work_gap: Dify and n8n offer visual drag-and-drop workflow builders that non-developers can use. A.R.C.H.I.E. currently requires code or CLI to configure workflows.
en.comp.cat_code_desc: AI-powered coding tools that write, edit, and debug code. Claude Code now has Agent Teams (Opus 4.6). Devin dropped to $20/mo. Cline hit 5M+ installs. Cursor crossed $500M+ ARR.
en.comp.cat_code_edge: A.R.C.H.I.E. is a full platform, not just a coding tool — coding is one of 46 modules. These tools write code; A.R.C.H.I.E. runs the whole operation.
en.comp.cat_code_gap: IDE integration, speed, and laser focus on developer experience. Coding agents are purpose-built for code; A.R.C.H.I.E. is purpose-built for everything.
en.comp.cat_ent_desc: Enterprise-grade platforms with teams, permissions, audit trails, and vendor support contracts.
en.comp.cat_ent_edge: Self-hosted, no vendor lock-in, own infrastructure. Full data sovereignty with zero cloud dependency.
en.comp.cat_ent_gap: Enterprise support contracts, compliance certifications from big-name auditors, and larger professional services teams.
en.comp.cat_self_desc: Open source alternatives focused on local-first AI. Privacy-first, community-driven, no vendor lock-in.
en.comp.cat_self_edge: Full platform vs individual tools. A.R.C.H.I.E. is unified — agents, models, security, operations, and fleet management in one system.
en.comp.cat_self_gap: Simpler setup, lighter weight, focused scope. These tools do one thing well; A.R.C.H.I.E. does 46 things.
en.comp.cat_acc_desc: This category doesn’t exist yet in the market. A.R.C.H.I.E. created it.
en.comp.cat_acc_edge: A platform that measures, audits, and proves its own improvement with data. Community skill library — auto-syncs 50+ quality-tested skills from GitHub to every node. Not just AI that works, but AI that gets better and can show the receipts.
en.comp.section_stack: How Many Tools to Match A.R.C.H.I.E.?
en.comp.stack_intro: One platform vs. a patchwork of nine
en.comp.stack_missing: And you’d still be missing agent welfare, warm-boot lifecycle, station-based dispatch, mission profiles, self-auditing codebase, Telegram escalation, and a unified knowledge base tying it all together.
en.comp.calculator_intro: Check the features you need. See what it costs to assemble them from separate tools vs. one Kytran subscription.
en.comp.building_intro: Transparency matters. Here’s what competitors do that A.R.C.H.I.E. doesn’t — yet. These are on our roadmap.
en.comp.build_a2a_desc: Google’s Agent-to-Agent standard lets agents from different platforms communicate. A.R.C.H.I.E.’s agents talk internally but can’t yet interop with external agent systems.
en.comp.build_scale_desc: Elastic cloud compute that scales to zero and spins up on demand. A.R.C.H.I.E. scales via fleet GPU nodes with OTA provisioning — hardware fleet scaling, not cloud auto-scaling. Trade-off: zero cloud bills, but requires physical hardware.
en.comp.build_scale_who: Who has it: Devin, Dify Cloud, OpenAI Assistants
en.comp.build_visual_desc: Drag-and-drop interface for non-developers to build AI workflows. A.R.C.H.I.E. requires code or CLI configuration. A visual builder would open the platform to broader teams.
en.comp.build_visual_who: Who has it: Dify, n8n, Langflow, Flowise
en.comp.build_durable_desc: Checkpoint-based execution that survives crashes and can resume from any point. A.R.C.H.I.E.’s jobs restart from the beginning if interrupted.
en.comp.section_ksm: Kytran Server Manager vs Vanta, Drata & More
en.comp.ksm_diff_remediation: No competitor offers scan + fix in one tool.
en.comp.ksm_diff_stig: DoD-grade compliance in an open-source package.
en.comp.ksm_diff_oss: Vanta & Drata are SaaS-only — your compliance data lives on their cloud.
en.comp.ksm_diff_ai: No compliance competitor has integrated AI analysis.
en.comp.cta_desc: One unified platform. 130+ agents. 17 departments. 90 autonomous jobs. Self-hosted, self-improving, and production-tested.
en.archie-code.hero-sub: Your AI-powered development companion. Built by Kytran Empowerment.
